chapter_title.ar: باب وَقْتِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي يَوْمِ الْغَيْمِ وَ الرِّيحِ وَ مَنْ صَلَّى لِغَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 8 – The time for Salāt during a cloudy and windy day, and the one who prays Salāt to other than the Qiblah

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/46)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/46)

parts.text: عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ وَ أَنْتَ عَلَى غَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ فَاسْتَبَانَ لَكَ أَنَّكَ صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى غَيْرِ الْقِبْلَةِ وَ أَنْتَ فِي وَقْتٍ فَأَعِدْ فَإِنْ فَاتَكَ الْوَقْتُ فَلَا تُعِدْ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Abdullah Aamir, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Abdul Rahman Bin Abu Abdullah,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When you pray Salāt and you are upon other than the Qiblah, and it becomes clear to you that you have prayed upon other than the Qiblah, and you are within the time, so repeat. But if the time is lost, so do not repeat’.
en.sarwar: 3. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amir, from Ali ibn Mahziyar from Fadalah ibn Ayyub, from ‘Abd al- Rahman ibn abu ‘Abd Allah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘You may perform a Salah (prayer) without facing al- Qiblah (al-Ka‘bah). You must perform it again, only if you realize it in time. If the time is gone, it is not obligatory for you to perform it again.’”
